description: Physical AI tool schema for Coral Dev Board (SL2619) FunctionGemma demo. Canonical mobile-actions format.

function.name: turn_on_lights
function.description: Turn on all RGB LEDs and the Neopixel strip to default white.

function.name: turn_off_lights
function.description: Turn off all RGB LEDs and the Neopixel strip.

function.name: set_led_color
function.description: Set the color of RGB LEDs on the HAT or Neopixel strip.

target.description: Which lights to set: 'all' (default), 'hat' (RGB LEDs on HAT), or 'strip' (Neopixels).

function.name: blink_lights
function.description: Blink LEDs a given number of times, optionally in a specific color and speed.

color.description: Color to blink. Default current color or white.

function.name: set_neopixel_pattern
function.description: Play an animated pattern on the Neopixel strip.

color.description: Color for patterns that need one (chase/fade/pulse/solid). Ignored for rainbow.

function.name: play_buzzer
function.description: Play a named pattern on the piezo buzzer on the HAT. The buzzer is binary GPIO on the Coral Dev Board (BUZZERn), so only timed patterns are supported — no tunable frequency.

function.name: set_alarm
function.description: Schedule an alarm to go off at a given time or after a duration. Alarm triggers buzzer + flashing LEDs.

duration.description: Relative time like '1 minute', '30 seconds', '2 hours'. Use this OR 'time', not both.

time.description: Absolute time HH:MM (24-hour). Use this OR 'duration', not both.

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the alarm.

function.name: cancel_alarm
function.description: Cancel a scheduled alarm. If no label is given, cancel all alarms.

label.description: Label of the alarm to cancel. Omit to cancel all.

function.name: list_alarms
function.description: List all currently scheduled alarms with their trigger times.

function.name: get_system_status
function.description: Report device system status (CPU load, memory, temperature, NPU utilization).

function.name: respond
function.description: Reply to the user in natural language without taking any physical action. Use this when the request is out of scope (no matching tool), ambiguous (needs clarification), purely conversational (greetings, thanks), or impossible on this device. Do NOT use this when any physical-action tool fits the request.

message.description: The natural-language reply to show to the user.
